Landscape Design & Outdoor Lighting

From Concept to Completion

Landscape Design & Build — Project Example

Landscape Design & Build

From new construction to landscape renovation, we combine art and science to create outdoor spaces that thrive in Florida's unique climate. Our design process includes 3D renderings or 2D plans so you see exactly what your landscape will look like before installation begins.

  • 3D Renderings & 2D Design Plans
  • Florida-Friendly Plant Selection
  • Custom Design Process (2-6 weeks)
  • Professional Installation
  • New Construction & Renovation

Outdoor Lighting — Project Example

Outdoor Lighting

Your landscape doesn't disappear at sunset. LED lighting transforms your property into an evening showcase with a wide variety of fixture and bulb options available.

  • LED Fixture Options
  • Wide Variety of Styles
  • Professional Installation
  • Nighttime Curb Appeal

How We Bring Your Vision to Life

A simple three-step process from first conversation to final walk-through.

1

Consultation

Consultation

We visit your property, discuss your vision, and recommend plants suited to your specific environment. Bring your ideas — we’ll bring the expertise.

2

Design

Design

Using 3D renderings or 2D plans, you’ll see exactly what your new landscape will look like before a single plant goes in the ground. Design takes 2-6 weeks.

3

Installation

Installation

Our team arrives on time, works efficiently, and leaves your property cleaner than we found it. Every time.

Certified. Insured. Family-Owned.

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about our landscape design and build process.

What areas do you serve for landscape design?

We serve Palm Beach Gardens, North Palm Beach, Jupiter, Tequesta, and Singer Island in Palm Beach County, Florida.

How long does the landscape design process take?

The design process typically takes 2-6 weeks, depending on the time of year, our current workload, and the size of your project. We provide 3D renderings or 2D design plans so you can see exactly what your landscape will look like.

What should I bring to the initial consultation?

We recommend bringing pictures of plants or ideas you'd like to incorporate, along with a copy of your property survey (usually found with your closing documents). During the consultation, we'll discuss your vision and suggest plants suited to your specific environment.

Do you use Florida-friendly plants?

Yes. Our team has completed the Green Industries Best Management Practices (GI-BMP) program through the University of Florida, which emphasizes Florida-friendly plants and "the right plant in the right location." This means healthier plants that require less irrigation and fewer pesticides.

Are you licensed and insured?

Yes. We are fully licensed and insured. We hold FNGLA Certified Horticulture Professional certification, FDACS Limited Commercial Landscape Maintenance and Fertilizer licenses, and are proud members of the Florida Landscape Management Association.

Do you work with new construction properties?

Absolutely. We work on both new construction landscaping and updating existing landscapes. From bare lots to landscape renovation, we'll create a customized design for your property.

Ready to Transform Your Outdoor Space?

Tell us about your project and we’ll respond within 1 business day with a free estimate.

Licensed & Insured. Serving Palm Beach County.
